course_description.overview: Granfondo route of approximately 130 km with 2000 m elevation gain, featuring the same climbs as the professional Volta ao Algarve, including the iconic Foia ascent in Monchique, through scenic Algarve landscapes on open roads.

key_segments.name: Foia (Alto da Fóia)
key_segments.description: Highest point in the region, iconic climb from the Volta ao Algarve pro race.

key_segments.name: Monchique Hills
key_segments.description: Significant elevation challenges in the Algarve interior.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Demanding hilly road course with substantial climbing, designed for fit cyclists seeking a pro-level challenge in a non-competitive format.

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Faro Airport (FAO), then drive or shuttle to Lagoa/Lagos area; event near Volta ao Algarve pro stages.
logistics.accommodation: Abundant in Algarve tourist region including Lagoa, Lagos, Portimão; book early due to concurrent pro race.
logistics.registration_details: Opens soon via official site; €40-50; deadline Feb 16, 2026 or sold out; max 1500 participants.
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bikes required; electronic chip timing; transport to Portugal standard for European events.
logistics.local_considerations: Open roads with controlled start; ages 19+ for Granfondo; festive atmosphere tied to Volta ao Algarve.

final_verdict.one_liner: Official amateur counterpart to Portugal's premier pro race, with pro climbs and Algarve beauty.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Ambitious road cyclists wanting pro-stage experience without elite competition.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the unique Volta ao Algarve tie-in, scenery, and value.
pro_tips: February weather mild but hilly - layer up.
